audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in block_wrappers.cc are safe: _sub_stream is always non-NULL (allocated via C++ new in constructor and reset_input_stream; new throws rather than returning NULL), and _output_buffer/_input_buffer are never directly dereferenced within this file — they are used only for pointer arithmetic or passed as arguments to external functions (snappy/LZ4) where any write occurs outside this translation unit. SCHECK guards in BackUp() (lines 48, 115) enforce that _backed_up_bytes > 0 implies the buffer was non-NULL, with the default log handler aborting on FATAL to prevent continuation past a failed check.
